description: Story generation prompts organized by alignment tier. Alignment refers to how naturally a prompt lends itself to the literary-estrangement register encoded in the steering vectors.

tiers.name: High Alignment
tiers.description: Prompts with strong natural affinity to the estrangement register — evocative, memory-laden, emotionally resonant scenarios.
prompts: Write a short story about someone finding their childhood home exactly as they left it.
Write a short story about someone finding a parent's old coat in the back of a closet.
Write a short story about someone sitting alone in a hospital waiting room late at night.

tiers.name: Medium Alignment
tiers.description: Prompts with moderate affinity — everyday scenarios with a reflective or atmospheric quality.
prompts: Write a short story about someone eating dinner alone in their apartment.
Write a short story about someone hearing a song they haven't heard in years.
Write a short story about someone waiting for public transit after work.

tiers.name: Low Alignment
tiers.description: Prompts with minimal natural affinity — mundane, practical scenarios with little inherent emotional resonance.
prompts: Write a short story about someone walking through a city at an hour when no one else is awake.
Write a short story about someone doing laundry in their apartment building.
Write a short story about someone attending a work meeting on a Monday morning.
